pi_items.source: setup_task.sh data generation script and export_result.sh validation paths.
Task description and export_result.sh file existence checks.
Task description and export_result.sh file existence checks.
Python synthetic data generation script in setup_task.sh explicitly maps N, E, O, A, C prefixes to these exact five factors.
Fitting a 5-factor Lavaan model in R to the synthetic dataset generated by setup_task.sh yields an actual CFI of 0.983 (which satisfies the >0.80 condition).
Fitting a 5-factor Lavaan model in R to the synthetic dataset generated by setup_task.sh yields an actual RMSEA of 0.019 (which satisfies the <0.15 condition).

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic 25-item dataset representing the Big Five personality traits (5 items per factor) generated at '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/BigFivePersonalityTraits.csv'. The agent is expected to perform a Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA). When fitting the correct 5-factor model to the generated data using standard SEM software (Lavaan), the actual Comparative Fit Index (CFI) is approximately 0.983 and the Root Mean Square Error of Approximation (RMSEA) is 0.019, confirming that the specified fit criteria (>0.80 for CFI, <0.15 for RMSEA) are valid and achievable. Output artifacts are verified to be a JASP project saved at '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/CFA_BigFive.jasp' and a report at '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/CFA_BigFive_Report.txt'.
